mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor demand and income expansion, which drives consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: post-recession recovery with sustained job creation
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive payroll growth periods do not produce significantly higher 20-day returns than negative payroll periods (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate above 4.5% signals monetary tightening that increases discount rates on future equity cash flows and raises corporate borrowing costs, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as markets reprice growth expectations downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, or Rank IC > -0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint expansion in housing starts and retail sales signals broad-based demand strength and consumer confidence. When both HOUST and RSAFS grow month-over-month simultaneously, equity valuations expand due to improved earnings visibility, producing positive 20-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:March 2013 HOUST 1191 thousand units, April 2013 RSAFS 380259 million dollars
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with synchronized demand acceleration
failure_condition: Rank IC of interaction term < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or joint HOUST+RSAFS growth periods do not produce significantly higher returns than single-series growth periods (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index declining month-over-month signals supply-side weakness and potential margin compression in manufacturing-heavy sectors. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings forecasts are revised downward.
failure_condition: Rank IC > -0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or INDPRO declines do not precede negative 60-day returns with statistical significance (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rising above 4.8% signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer purchasing power. This triggers negative equity repricing as forward earnings growth expectations decline, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances when UNRATE > 4.8%, or Rank IC > -0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI rising above 2.5% year-over-year combined with elevated federal funds rate signals monetary policy constraint. This dual tightening reduces real money supply and equity valuations, producing negative 60-day forward returns as growth expectations compress.
regime_hint: stagflation risk or monetary policy tightening cycle
failure_condition: Rank IC of interaction term < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or high inflation + high rates periods do not produce significantly lower 60-day returns than low inflation periods (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured by GDPC1 accelerating above 2.5% annualized signals robust economic expansion and rising corporate earnings. This produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as equity risk premiums compress in a growth-supportive environment.
regime_hint: mid-to-late cycle expansion with above-trend growth
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or periods of GDPC1 growth > 2.5% do not produce significantly higher 60-day returns than slower growth periods (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts declining month-over-month while unemployment rises simultaneously signals demand destruction and forward-looking economic weakness. This joint deterioration produces negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as markets immediately reprice recession risk.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 3% month-over-month AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of interaction term > -0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.
